programme.desc: Robert feeds 44 hungry alligators, all at the same time. Terri helps examine Kaitlyn the tiger and makes a thrilling discovery.

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: A drunken man wreaks havoc on Sam Rayburn Lake; Wardens Randy Sovall and Sean Reneau deal with the aftermath; Warden Mike Boone tracks down the culprits responsible for an illegal dumpsite; a gator is residing in a familys pond.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr. Jeff and his team of 30 experts preside over one of the busiest animal clinics in the U.S.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Nichols treats a cat injured in a fight with the dog next door, and a class of fourth-graders count on Dr Jeff to save their pet hedgehog.

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.sub-title: Home is Where the Dog Is
programme.desc: One of Earls favourite senior dogs has a chance at finding a forever home. Plus, Tia heads to a shelter in Louisiana to help with a hound theyve taken in.

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Wanting to give the senior dogs a special place, Lizzy, Tania, and Mariah create a retirement centre at one of the warehouses. And, Lizzy rescues a dog that has one of the worst skin conditions she has ever seen.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Steev and Laura have been dating for seven years, but Steevs cat Darkness is tearing them apart. Can Jackson ease their woes?

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Peggy is a Real Housewife of Orange County. Being a housewife, shes used to catfights, except shes now dealing with one involving her real cat.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.sub-title: A Treehouse Fit for a Viking
programme.desc: Pete takes on his first international build in Norway. Working with local builder, Frode, the 24 ft high retreat gives stunning views of a nearby lake.

programme.title: The Gamechangers: Inventing the World
programme.desc: Two teams of young engineers each believe they can revolutionise global transport. A high-speed aerodynamic bike and a solar-powered car are put to the test.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations reveal why elephants have trunks. Plus, why do some animals live so long, and why do cats purr?.

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: Forrest scours lakes and rivers of both metropolitan and rural Vietnam in search of one of the last living specimens of the giant softshell turtle.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Deep in the Alaskan bush lives a family who was born and raised wild. Billy and Amy Brown - and their seven children - live off the grid and rely on each other to survive.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett create a temporary tank for basketball star Dwyane Wade; a North Carolina restaurant requests a burger- and sushi-filled aquarium.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Jane and Charlotte trek through bear country to protect their herd. Three generations of Kilchers build a shack on Perl Island and Otto has a surprise for Charlotte.

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: As Jeremy gets his farmstead up and running, unfinished business gets in the way. Plus, bears threaten Jenny and her newborn whilst stranded on an island in a storm.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Mireya and Ronny return to where they heard whistles and saw glowing eyes. Meanwhile, Mireya agrees to be bait in order to lure out Bigfoot.

programme.title: Loch Ness Monster: New Evidence
programme.desc: Top scientists reveal how a revolutionary study and advanced DNA technology solved the mystery of the legendary Loch Ness monster.

programme.title: Azad Desh Ka Andha Kanoon
programme.desc: A brash and honest cop is torn apart between his duties and saving his family when his own brother is caught in a blue-film scam.

programme.title: Prithipal Singh... A Story
programme.desc: Based on a real story of the great Indian hockey player Prithipal Singh, who was a survivor of partition.

programme.title: Samay: When Time Strikes
programme.desc: Assistant Commissioner of Police Malvika Chauhan battles time to solve the various murders occurring in the city.

programme.title: Main Intequam Loonga
programme.desc: When the Agnihotris bring home a woman named Ganga, Kumar discovers that she is his biological mother and his father, Ajay Kumar, was the National Champion in boxing, who was killed by three men for refusing to lose a fight.

programme.title: Devta
programme.desc: A king loses his sight and needs a `Nagi Jyothi (`serpent light) found in `Sarpaloka (serpent world). A young man undertakes the journey of going to the netherworld and getting the magic light from the hood of a snake.

programme.title: Krishna Arjun
programme.desc: After news of his mothers death, Arjun travels home to find that his brother Krishna has also been arrested for rape and murder and he sets out to find out why.

programme.title: Viduthalai Part 1
programme.desc: A policeman is conflicted between duty and morality when his department begins a brutal campaign to capture a local activist.

programme.desc: After getting out from prison, Randhir plans to avenge his brothers death, without knowing that he himself could endanger his very life, as well the life of his girlfriend, Mala.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Using 70 years of BBC history archive film, Professor Alice Roberts uncovers how the iconic ancient monument of Stonehenge has been interpreted, argued over and debated by some of Britains leading historians and archaeologists. She reveals how new discoveries would discredit old theories, how astronomers and geologists became involved in the story and why, even after centuries of study, theres still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge.

programme.title: Julian Barnes: Beyond the Page
programme.desc: Julian Barnes: How a very unconfident man went on to be feted as one of the best of British novelists of the 1980s. Scaling the heights of literary greatness with works such as Flauberts Parrot and The Sense of an Ending, hes now penned his final novel. The award-winning writer talks to the BBCs culture editor, Katie Razzall about his career, his thoughts on death and saying farewell to his novels and readers.

BBC Radio 1 Xtras DJ Target presents exclusive backstage coverage, including all the glitz and glamour of the red carpet, alongside performances and interviews with the likes of Lauryn Hill, Olivia Dean, Myles Smith, Tiwa Savage, Jamaican dancehall star Shenseea and hip-hop legend Slick Rick.

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive ventures to the eastern tip of Cuba to explore its oldest city, Baracoa. He builds his own traditional bamboo raft to experience for himself how people got around in a place that had no proper road access until the 1960s.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Julian Barnes: Beyond the Page
programme.desc: Julian Barnes: How a very unconfident man went on to be feted as one of the best of British novelists of the 1980s. Scaling the heights of literary greatness with works such as Flauberts Parrot and The Sense of an Ending, hes now penned his final novel. The award-winning writer talks to the BBCs culture editor, Katie Razzall about his career, his thoughts on death and saying farewell to his novels and readers.

programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan heads to the Rocky Mountains in search of wild horses. Finding two stallions battling over a herd, Gordon immerses himself in their world, hoping to win their trust.

programme.title: City Lights
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at `City Lights, the iconic sitcom that captured the spirit of 1980s Scotland. Surviving cast members Elaine C Smith, Jonathan Watson, Dave Anderson and the shows creator, Bob Black look back at the series magical moments.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: With Boabby out of the game, its up to Jack and Victor to pull a team together for the Walking Football tournament and set their sights on winning that much-needed new pitch for their local community.

programme.desc: Every ounce of Scottish football from the SPFL to the Sunday league ringers. It means everything, it means nothing, and this is the view of the fans on the terrace.

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish
programme.desc: Wendy Wason, Chris MacArthur-Boyd, Jamie McDonald, Ashley Storrie and Janey Godley, amongst others, remember Family Life; dinner time, family phrases and getting told off.

programme.desc: In a future society, people are divided into factions based on their personalities. After a young woman learns she is a Divergent and will never fit into any one group, she uncovers a conspiracy to destroy all those like her.

programme.title: The Divergent Series: Insurgent
programme.desc: Beatrice Prior must confront her inner demons and continue her fight against a powerful alliance that threatens to tear her society apart.

programme.title: The Divergent Series: Allegiant
programme.desc: As a ruthless battle threatens humanity, Tris and Four journey beyond the wall that encloses Chicago to find a peaceful solution for their embroiled city.

programme.title: My Mates A Bad Date
programme.desc: Khalil never remembers his dates names, so he calls them all baby girl to skirt around it. Obsessed with looking good, he checks himself out in the reflection of a spoon every 20 minutes on dinner dates.

programme.desc: From Bletchley Parks wartime secrets to a beloved brooch and the art of wig making - join Dom and Will on another restoration road trip.

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: The four finalists return for the last time to the Great Hall, ready to be put through their chess-playing paces like never before. Who will lift the winners trophy?

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Clive has visited Rome with his wife Catherine and his mates, as well as for the BBC - this time, he heads out of the centre and up one of the citys seven hills to the rarely visited neighbourhood of Monteverde, where he talks football with a butcher and puts his Caribbean cooking knowledge to good use. He also comes to the surprising realisation that if he ever moves to Rome, hell likely take up cricket.

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: A young Taiwanese pangolin searches for a mate before the breeding season ends. Pangolins are the worlds most trafficked animal, so it is crucial he finds a female in time. After facing a large cobra, he reaches the very edge of his known territory.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A second chance to see Michael Aspel and the experts when they return to the gardens of Cliveden in Buckinghamshire and discover a ladies bureau brought in by a relative of Joshua Reynolds, an eccentrically large barometer, an unusual McMickey Mouse and a silver cruet set made by one of the great silversmiths, worth £30,000.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by experts Charlie Ross and Tracy Martin at Oxford Universitys beautiful Sheldonian Theatre. Some interesting pieces come up including a stylish Rolex watch and an unwanted inkwell. Paul hears the story behind a book that originally took 70 years to complete - the Oxford English Dictionary. All the items in the show are sent to a packed saleroom in Watlington, Oxfordshire.

programme.desc: Does a home security video show proof that UFOs are messing with our technology? Did a species of carnivorous marsupials thought to be long extinct just get caught on camera? Did a visitor at a historic cemetery in Savannah capture a ghost child?

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Modern imaging technology and analysis from military and intelligence professionals, as well as leading scientists, investigate mysteries from strange geological occurrences to odd man-made structures as seen by more than 4,000 orbiting satellites.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Its the only unsolved air piracy case in history. In 1971, a man hijacks a Northwest Orient Airlines flight, demanding four parachutes and $200,000. He jumps out of the plane with the money and is never seen again.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Its one of the most famous prehistoric monuments on Earth -- a mysterious arrangement of stones that have adorned the English countryside for millennia. But to this day, nobody knows how the landmark was built or why.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: After seizing power in 1933, Adolf Hitler began systematically transforming a peaceful German population into a ruthless war machine. Experts investigate the innovative methods used by the Nazis to indoctrinate and control the will of its citizens.

programme.title: Holy Marvels With Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: Its one of lifes most confounding mysteries: What happens to us after we die? From heaven and hell to resurrection and reincarnation, well explore the remarkable possibilities of life after death.

programme.desc: A journey to some of the mysteries of how the early Celts emerged and expanded, from collapsed salt mines, to bizarre rituals and lost cities.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Even after centuries of study, the human mind remains as inscrutable as ever. Take a look if the quintessential human machine, the brain, be even more powerful than humans realise.

programme.title: The UnXplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: A look at how some people live through the impossible, such as plane crashes, freezing temperatures, or performing superhuman feats while in danger. These remarkable tales of survival force others to reconsider what is known about the will to live.

programme.desc: Ufologists discuss what UFOs may be, where they may originate and what may be their agenda.

programme.desc: Duggee is putting up a picture of himself and the old friends he grew up with, who are just like family to him. This inspires the Squirrels to take a photo of their own, so they take turns getting smartened up for the camera with varying degrees of success. After a number of experimental poses, they eventually settle on the perfect composition, until Tag spots Enid - shes part of the family too, so shouldnt she be in the photo?

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag hurts her foot on the job, Vida is there to help fix it. However, Zigzag still has to deliver her packages in time for Vidas surprise Vet Day party.

programme.desc: Supertato and Evil Pea go head-to-head on a game show to try and win a speedboat.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasys turns detective when his harmonica disappears.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo need to move a crashed meteor. But every time Mojo goes near the big rock, moving turns to grooving. How can you move a meteor if you cant stop dancing?

programme.title: Bluey

programme.desc: Ruby and Finns seeds have started to grow, but Rafis cherry seed shows no change. Feeling left out, she makes a wish, and a giant cherry tree appears! Its exciting at first, but when the wish ends, everything disappears. Rafi feels sad, until Doris shares a story about planting an apple tree when she was little. Rafi realises that nature likes to take its time, and that waiting can bring something special.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An old high-school friend of Monicas is invited to the Thanksgiving dinner with the gang, but Monica is unaware that he isnt exactly fond of Rachel. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Dates Rachel

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Takes a Bath
programme.desc: As Joey agonises over his secret affection for Rachel, Monica mistakenly thinks that he is infatuated with Phoebe. Meanwhile, Ross and Rachel promise not to try to find out the sex of their unborn child, but Rachel cant resist having a peek inside the doctors confidential folder. Also, Monica urges a reluctant Chandler to enjoy the soothing pleasures of a scented bath, but realises that shes created a tub monster.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Secret Closet
programme.desc: Joey rushes pregnant Rachel to hospital when she experiences pains. When Ross feels left out, Joey makes a gallant suggestion. Meanwhile, Chandler is surprised to discover what is behind the locked door of Monicas secret cupboard, and Phoebe gets the impression that Monica is enjoying one of her massages a little too much.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birthing Video
programme.desc: Monica prepares for a sexy Valentines Day, but Chandler is too shell-shocked after accidentally viewing Rachels birthing video. Meanwhile, Phoebe gives Joey the worlds most cheerful dog.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Tells Rachel
programme.desc: A guilt-ridden Joey finally confesses his love for Rachel to the flabbergasted Ross, while Chandler becomes nervous when Phoebes handsome new boyfriend Don shares numerous personality traits with Monica.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is caught in a compromising position by Amys parents when they turn up unannounced at her apartment.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert is put in charge of selecting his wedding invitations, Ray suggests he intentionally botch the assignment so that Amy will let him off the hook with their wedding plans.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond is horrified when he thinks his `cookie nemesis flirted with him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets annoyed with Ray when he seems pleased that shes picked one of her attractive friends to replace her if she dies.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gives a bridal shower for Amy, but her good intentions get sidetracked by Maries overzealous participation.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra wind up in a two-week standoff over a suitcase.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Marie give Raymond a guilt trip when they find out that the bachelor party he hosted for Robert was a huge disappointment.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Peter attempts to bury the hatchet between himself, Robert and Ray, Robert suspects hes up to no good and assigns Ray to keep an eye on him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Amy exchange vows in a wedding ceremony that has the Barone family tradition written all over it.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra convinces Ray to let her join him on the golf course after she once again realises that they dont have fun together anymore.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Michael stays home from school for two days in a row, Ray suspects he simply has homework he hasnt finished.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie instantly tries to get the upper hand in her relationship with her new daughter-in-law when Amy and Robert return from their honeymoon.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray are annoyed at the over-the-top affection Robert and Amy are expressing towards each other.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra persuades Ray to hire Gianni to do some DIY work in their kitchen, but they both soon regret it due to their friends laziness.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: After Amy invites her brother Peter to stay for dinner, he makes himself at home on the couch and never leaves.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tells Marie a little white lie that snowballs out of control.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: Features music from multi award-winning rap superstar Tinie Tempah and an acrobatic performance from the Peres Brothers Group. Plus, one unsuspecting member of the public gets the surprise of their life when they become the unexpected star of the show.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tea Leaves
programme.desc: In an effort to restore normality to their friendship, Rachel tells Joey a big lie about her unborn child. Meanwhile, Phoebes tea leaves reveal that she will soon encounter the man of her dreams.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe brings her annoyingly upbeat boyfriend to Mr and Mrs Gellers 35th wedding anniversary party. Meanwhile, Ross is astounded when his parents ask him and the pregnant Rachel to pretend to be married, in order to avoid a potential scandal.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is thrilled when his name is used in a `Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle. He is reluctant to let the magazine do a profile of him, but Rachel tries to persuade him to reconsider so that he can mention her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower
programme.desc: Rachel panics when she realises how unprepared she is to care for an infant. She reluctantly agrees to allow her frustrating mother Sandra to move in to help her, upsetting Ross. Meanwhile, Monica tries to atone for forgetting to invite Sandra to the baby shower, and Chandler and Ross exhaust themselves by repeatedly playing a confusing game called Bamboozle to help Joey prepare to audition as a game show host.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cooking Class
programme.desc: When Monicas cooking at the restaurant receives a harsh write-up in a newspaper, she goes to a criticism class taught by the reviewer, hoping to convince him to reconsider his opinion. Meanwhile, Rachel gets upset when a cute salesgirl flirts with Ross while he and Rachel are shopping for baby supplies, and nervous about a major job interview, Chandler receives some questionable coaching from Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late
programme.desc: When Rachels pregnancy goes beyond the expected delivery date, the pain makes her increasingly irritable and willing to try anything that might speed up the babys arrival. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe bet on the date of the birth, while Joey can only take one guest to the premiere of his film, and chooses Chandler. Unfortunately, Chandler falls asleep during the screening.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross, but to her dismay she is put in a semi-private room and then, the heavily pregnant Janice, is allocated to share the room with her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Has a Baby
programme.desc: Rachel waits in the hospital maternity ward with an anxious Ross. Numerous other pregnant women are wheeled off to give birth while Rachels painful labour continues. Elsewhere in the hospital, Phoebe is attracted to a handsome patient, Cliff, and asks Joey to pose as Dr Drake Ramoray to interrogate Cliff about his background.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One Proposes
programme.desc: Due to a misunderstanding, Rachel finds herself engaged to Joey instead of Ross shortly after giving birth to her and Rosss baby girl. Meanwhile, Joey cant help taking a look as Rachel tries unsuccessfully to nurse her baby. Inspired to have a baby of their own, Monica and Chandler retire to a hospital cupboard for a little privacy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joeys futile attempts to gain Rosss forgiveness for mistakenly asking Rachel to marry him only result in emergency hospitalisation. Meanwhile, an exhausted Rachel desperately searches for a solution to end baby Emmas non-stop crying.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartmans racist ways lead him to weave some unexpected, cupid style, magic. Meanwhile, Kyle is less than happy with what the rumour mill has to say about him.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Randy is so enraged by South Park Elementary changing the rules of American Football he sarcastically suggests more changes, and Butters creates a sports drink.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: As Cartman finally admits that he is yet another statistic regarding obese children in America he purchases a mobility scooter insisting that it is his right to drive around on it. programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman is having nightmares that depict him as Brad Pitt in World War Z, but its not zombies hes fighting. All hell breaks loose in South Park when he takes action!

programme.desc: Rachels constant calls to Emmas paediatrician force him to reject her as a patient. Meanwhile, Chandler plans to move to Tulsa for work, but worries about whether or not career-minded Monica is still willing to join him, and Joey and Phoebe agree to find each other companions for a double date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe fears losing her latest prospective boyfriend Mike and her self-doubt is not helped by Ross careless comments. Meanwhile, Chandler is in Tulsa on a work assignment, and when Monica surprises him in his hotel room, she mistakenly thinks that he has a sexual fetish for sharks.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Phoebes Birthday Dinner
programme.desc: While Phoebe and Joey wait at a fancy restaurant for their friends arrival to celebrate Phoebes birthday, Rachel is delayed because she is afraid to leave Emma with a babysitter. Monica and Chandler also fail to arrive on time after having a row about Chandlers urge to resume smoking and then giving in to other urges.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Male Nanny
programme.desc: Ross mocks Rachels new male nanny, and Chandler becomes upset when Monica says that somebody from her job is the funniest guy shes ever met.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss Inappropriate Song
programme.desc: When Chandlers former romantic rival intends to sell his apartment, insecure Chandler persuades Joey to help him secretly inspect it and the guys are shocked to discover an old videotape featuring Monica having sex with Richard.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Other Sister
programme.desc: On the night before Thanksgiving, Rachels self-centred younger sister Amy ruins everyones holiday dinner with her uncensored observations. Meanwhile, Monica obsessively protects her good wedding china, and Joey forgets to join his television cast mates in the big Thanksgiving parade.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Phone Number
programme.desc: Rachel impulsively gives a handsome restaurant patron her telephone number, and worries that hell call when Ross is in their apartment. Meanwhile, Phoebes boyfriend Mike and Ross desperately try to find something in common to talk about when they babysit Emma, and Chandler returns from Tulsa to visit Monica, but must hide from Joey after rejecting his offer of free basketball tickets.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Christmas in Tulsa
programme.desc: Chandler is forced to remain in Tulsa over the Christmas period due to a major deadline at work, and Monica fears the worst when she finds out that he will be joined in Tulsa by a beautiful colleague Wendy. While he works, Chandler recalls some of the more embarrassing moments from previous holidays shared with his friends.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Goes Back to Work
programme.desc: When Rachel returns to her office to show off baby Emma, she is surprised to find handsome temp Gavin has been doing her job while she has been away on maternity leave. Their immediate war of wills prompts Rachel to make a rash decision.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe and Mike parent baby rats born in her cabinet, Rachel gets steamed after Monica invites a rival to her birthday bash, and Joey likes Emmas new nanny.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While Rachel tries to sort out her mixed feelings for her handsome colleague Gavin, Ross recruits Chandler to help him meet attractive women and make Rachel jealous. Meanwhile, Phoebe regrets bringing Monica to a karaoke night when she takes the microphone and delights in the applause, unaware that the men in the audience are cheering because they can see through her dress, and Joey submits to painful eyebrow waxing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Blind Dates
programme.desc: Joey and Phoebe conspire to arrange awful dates for both Rachel and Ross, hoping that appreciation of their former relationship will push them back together. Meanwhile, the ovulating Monica wants to get intimate with Chandler.

programme.desc: The side-splitting grand finale of Lee Evans 100 date sell-out UK tour at the Edinburgh Playhouse.

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Goes Back to Work
programme.desc: When Rachel returns to her office to show off baby Emma, she is surprised to find handsome temp Gavin has been doing her job while she has been away on maternity leave. Their immediate war of wills prompts Rachel to make a rash decision.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe and Mike parent baby rats born in her cabinet, Rachel gets steamed after Monica invites a rival to her birthday bash, and Joey likes Emmas new nanny.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While Rachel tries to sort out her mixed feelings for her handsome colleague Gavin, Ross recruits Chandler to help him meet attractive women and make Rachel jealous. Meanwhile, Phoebe regrets bringing Monica to a karaoke night when she takes the microphone and delights in the applause, unaware that the men in the audience are cheering because they can see through her dress, and Joey submits to painful eyebrow waxing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Blind Dates
programme.desc: Joey and Phoebe conspire to arrange awful dates for both Rachel and Ross, hoping that appreciation of their former relationship will push them back together. Meanwhile, the ovulating Monica wants to get intimate with Chandler.

programme.title: Lee Evans: Live in Scotland
programme.desc: The side-splitting grand finale of Lee Evans 100 date sell-out UK tour at the Edinburgh Playhouse.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: The team is joined by actor Brett Dier as they play games including Party Quirks, Duet, Dubbing, Scenes from a Hat, Living Scenery and Hoedown.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Enjoy another dose of improv chaos, incl. Weird Newscasters with grouchy grandma Gary, Ryan reviewing great human inventions, and Wayne presenting the weather as a rock opera.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Wayne, Ryan and Colin sing a touching Doo Wop tribute to Rachel who died in a tragic breakfast accident. Scenes From A Hat covers everything from flight attendants to speaking body parts.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by celebrity guests Nick Grimshaw, Peter Serafinowicz, Daniel Sloss and Rachel Riley to debate the hot topics of the week.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by Josh Widdicombe, Dr Christian Jessen, Kimberley Walsh and Russell Kane to debate the hot topics of the week by examining surveys, opinion polls and statistics.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre: Showtime
programme.desc: McIntyre returns on top form with razor sharp observations and has the crowd in stitches with talk his wife will not be happy with! Legendary British stand up! Recorded at the O2 in London.

programme.title: Rosie Tries to Help
programme.desc: Rosie Jones joined by Ed Gamble to help the elderly, Tom Allen on male emotions, Ivo Graham on kids, and Phil Wang on stress.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: In the logging world, big wood equals big money. Its a tough job for the crews logging and transporting the biggest, most valuable timber on Earth.

programme.title: The Last Woodsmen
programme.desc: Facing the devastation of losing the Forever Claim, Jared makes a desperate deal with the powerful businessman who outbid him -- but new challenges could destroy his logging season before it begins.

programme.desc: A couple race to finish a modern, off-grid lodge overlooking Wisconsins south shores of Lake Superior.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys struggle to revive an inherited Wyoming hill country homestead with a tragic legacy; to bring water to the property, Marty must solve the mystery of an untapped well; Misty and Matt combine forces on one of their most ambitious builds.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Richard and the Cogs restore a 1947 Delahaye and a Jaguar XK120 for Salon Prive. While Richard explores the future of motoring in a Bentley powered by synthetic fuels at Goodwood, a Lancia taken on to help leopards threatens to derail plans.

programme.title: Outback Car Hunters
programme.desc: The guys revive a Bavarian beauty when they rescue a 1970 BMW Neue Klasse. A desert dash unearths two Mini Moke shells that could make a big profit.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Paul and Drew set their sights on a Lancia Montecarlo. This classic car needs serious work, but the guys are still willing to offer £7,000 for it.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Father-son duo Mike and Vinnie Elliott embark on a risky cash run to salvage cars, Lyle Lucas delivers vital supplies to the remote Oak Valley community, and Mark Talbot sets off on a high-stakes mission to deliver $500,000 Wagyu beef cattle.

programme.title: Ant Anstead: Born Mechanic
programme.desc: Ant hopes restoring and selling a 1980 Triumph Dolomite Sprint will help kickstart his farmhouse renovation. But will rust problems erode his profit?

programme.title: Motor Pickers
programme.desc: A schoolteacher wants a car to match his outdoorsy lifestyle. With towing the top priority, Helen and Paul look at vehicles with big load spaces and pulling power.

programme.title: Motor Pickers
programme.desc: Working with a whopping £100,000 budget, Paul and Helen hunt for high-end luxury cars for a champion weightlifter - and he already owns a Bentley!

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead work together to give new life to run-down classic automobiles to resell them at a profit. Working on a tight budget, Mike must find a vehicle for Ant to fix, but that isnt always the easiest task.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Sam and Henry travel to Leicestershire to meet former motorbike racer, Eddie Roberts. His collection impresses the boys and even includes a rare 1962 Greeves Sportsman.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Motorcycle fanatic Henry Cole travels around the country with his mechanic mate Sam Lovegrove nosing around peoples sheds and garages in the hope of finding some automotive bargains.

programme.title: Ant Anstead: Born Mechanic
programme.desc: Ant has found an iconic VW Campervan thats been abandoned mid-restoration. The interior needs a full refit, plus a suspension overhaul.

programme.title: Holden Bros Restos
programme.desc: The brothers stance and drop a Cummins diesel into an 85 Dodge and turn it into a Sunday shredder.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: After a heartfelt memorial for his late father, Yogi embarks on a gruelling five-day journey across Australia, Mel Michelsen tackles a tight deadline, and Mike and Vinnie continue their journey toward Halls Creek.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Car dealing legends Mike and Edd are back in their west coast workshop in the US, searching, buying, restoring, testing and selling more great motors in the American market.

programme.title: Ant Anstead: Born Mechanic
programme.desc: Ant has found an iconic VW Campervan thats been abandoned mid-restoration. The interior needs a full refit, plus a suspension overhaul.

programme.title: Richard Hammonds Workshop

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: With updates well underway on custom car guru Joe Martins prized 65 Pontiac Catalina, Joe and the boys at Martin Bros. Customs give the ol choppy-doo to a 41 DeSoto that has seen better days.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead work together to give new life to run-down classic automobiles to resell them at a profit. Working on a tight budget, Mike must find a vehicle for Ant to fix, but that isnt always the easiest task.

programme.desc: The 67 Coronet build continues. Vinnie wanted to go all out on this build and Dave has done some of his most innovative design work to date.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: A 1941 Chevy has been delayed by numerous issues. Now, its ready for a fresh lick of paint, new wiring and custom interior to fully upgrade this classic car.

programme.title: Kindig Customs

programme.title: Three Men Four Wheels
programme.desc: Racing driver Marino Franchitti adores the Ferrari F40. He hopes a few fast laps can convince a sceptical Drew Pritchard of this supercars prowess.

programme.title: Three Men Four Wheels
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard has been obsessed with the Stovebolt Special for decades. He takes this extraordinary car for a ride with racing driver Marino Franchitti.

programme.title: Bad Chad Customs
programme.desc: In rural Canning, Nova Scotia, Chad Hiltz runs a modest auto body shop with a legendary reputation. A custom car artist and master builder, Bad Chad and his tight-knit team can turn metal from the junkyards into award-winning cars.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Linda Widdups monster hay convoy races to deliver hay supplies to fire ravaged farmers, Josh Carter hauls a monster oversize convoy, and Steve Grahame transports a triple trailer load whilst battling against Mother Nature.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Johnny and Graeme face a critical oil leak during the Finke Desert Race, Jeff Fulwood undertakes a mercy mission to remote Nhulunbuy, and brothers Mason and Jacob Pakai haul a heavy load over the treacherous Great Dividing Range.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: A 30-truck charity hay convoy delivers supplies to drought-stricken King Island, rookie Harrison Hooper tackles his toughest challenge yet, and Irish trucker Liam OConnor braves the outbacks harshest conditions.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Father-son duo Mike and Vinnie Elliott embark on a risky cash run to salvage cars, Lyle Lucas delivers vital supplies to the remote Oak Valley community, and Mark Talbot sets off on a high-stakes mission to deliver $500,000 Wagyu beef cattle.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: Bill goes back to his roots building mini-trucks with a 2023 Polaris Ranger, adding a custom camper and dancing bed; Ekstensive has built its share of ground-up builds over the years, but nothing like the metalwork on the 1952 Wrecker.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.sub-title: Denali Lift and Hot Rod Wrecker
programme.desc: What was already one of Ekstensives biggest ground-up builds ever goes even bigger on the 52 tow truck, as Bill and his team fabricate a custom hot rod wrecker truck unlike any other; the team takes the stock out of a 2023 GMC Denali.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: John Cena surprises the shop with parts of an iconic 37 Bugatti Type 57; its Bills turn to surprise John with an audacious design choice; a stock 23 GMC Denali goes bigger, badder and dually.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: The team is called in to rescue a 1969 Plymouth Road Runner. The owner has been busy helping others give the car the attention it needs.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: A man sacrificed the engine of his 1965 Mercury Comet to help his wife out, and now she wants to return the favour by enlisting the team to restore the car to glory.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: After 30 years in storage, a Triumph Spitfire sputters back on the road.

programme.title: Shed and Buried

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh follows in the footsteps of Indiana Jones as he searches in Israel and Ethiopia for the lost Ark of the Covenant, the sacred relic said to contain the Ten Commandments and possess extraordinary powers.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Millers team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Somerset, property developer Norman and Dees Indian cuisine proves a little too spicy. Ashley lets Sophie down by wearing a creased shirt, and Brett and Vicky serve up an American menu.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cornwall, artistic Zaya and Damian serve a modern British menu. Property developers Kelly and Jon show off their refurbished mansion. And Emma and Gavin host a fun-filled Cornish night.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan are here to help Nick and Julie leave their small, cluttered home for a larger space. Though first, the brothers need to revamp the cramped space in order for it to be ready for the market.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Fantasy fan Evan plans to wage war on his wedding, but his emotional bride-to-be Zoe wants a low-key affair. The question remains as to whether this noble groom will make his bride feel like a queen.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hapless Liam wants his greatest passion to take flight with a darts-inspired wedding. Will his arrows extravaganza hit the target, or prove a load of old bull?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based Forman Mills, a discount clothing superstore, goes incognito among his own staff posing as a contestant on a television reality show.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stella and Dot CEO Jessica Herrin goes under cover to find the real gems working in her company, and learns one employees true feelings about corporate management.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Lelas is a fine dining restaurant in Pomona, California, run by inexperienced owner Lela and young chef Ricky. The fatal combination of her ineptitude and his arrogance has resulted in a business so rocky that its about to close down for good.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sebastians, a pizzeria in the heart of movie land in Burbank, Los Angeles, and goes head-to-head with the owner to simplify the menu.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Peep Show

programme.desc: First to host in Oxfordshire is amateur MC James, and his more sensible half Heather. Day two sees Marjie and Matt push the boat out with a Titanic-themed evening. Finally, Tutty and Sonja try to impress with their refined countryside dining.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Excitable groom Jonny plans an adrenaline-fuelled wedding for his bride-to-be Kate, who favours something simple. Will Jonnys big plans send Kate over the edge?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Yanis wants his big day to be a towering hit, with plans featuring the Eiffel tower. When the wedding plans are leaked, the couple are left facing the consequences.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Rugby-loving Wes plans the biggest wedding ever, in front of 26,000 people at a Premiership rugby match. But bride-to-be Charlotte had something more intimate in mind for her big day.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Self-conscious groom Richard plans a first-class trip for his bride. He has to try and meet straight-talking Latrices big hopes for a deluxe event.

programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay works his magic on a New York state eatery that has been renovated but still has horrible service and food.

programme.title: The Card Counter
programme.desc: William Tell is a gambler and former serviceman who sets out to reform a young man seeking revenge on a mutual enemy from their past. Tell just wants to play cards. His spartan existence on the casino trail is shattered when he is approached by Cirk, a vulnerable and angry young man seeking help to execute his plan for revenge on a military colonel. Tell sees a chance at redemption through his relationship with Cirk.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest
programme.desc: Lively reworking of the exploits of Robin Hood who foils the villainous Sheriff of Nottingham and the Earl of Newarks dastardly plan to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury.

programme.title: Turner and Hooch
programme.desc: Turner is a meticulously groomed, excruciatingly well-organised detective working in a small California coastal town. When local character John McIntyre is murdered by drug smugglers, the only witness is McIntyres slobbery, smelly mutt Hooch. Turner, who despises dogs in general and Hooch in particular, is compelled to put the cantankerous dog up as his house guest.

programme.title: The Incredible Shrinking Man
programme.desc: An encounter with a strange mist leaves Scott Carey with a big problem , hes shrinking and fast. Can he learn to live in miniature? Director Jack Arnold broke new ground with his innovative special effects in this classic science fiction flick.

programme.title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
programme.desc: On a journey to find the centre of the Earth, a scientist and a student find themselves in the lost city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Dog
programme.desc: With a dog named Lulu by his side, Army Ranger Briggs races down the Pacific Coast to make it to a soldiers funeral on time. Along the way, Briggs and Lulu drive each other completely crazy, break a handful of laws and narrowly evade death.

After crashing on an unknown planet, two survivors quickly discover theyre actually stranded on Earth, 65 million years ago. With only one chance at a rescue, they must make their way across an unknown terrain riddled with prehistoric creatures.

programme.title: Horizon Line
programme.desc: A former couple board a single-engine plane for a routine and casual flight to their friends tropical island wedding. But within minutes after takeoff, their pilot suffers a fatal heart attack, leaving them with no clue how to land the plane.

programme.title: My Heart Cant Beat Unless You Tell It To
programme.desc: Two mysterious siblings find themselves at odds over care for their frail and sickly younger brother.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson, and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. They learn how mead is made on Holy Island, before James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, and they both enjoy a Puffin cruise at Cockett Island. Theres a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers continue their northern odyssey in the county of Northumberland. Its an area that Si knows well, having holidayed there many times throughout his childhood, and he is keen to introduce Dave to some of the producers and chefs he has got to know over the years. Starting in the seaside town of Amble, where they eat fresh seafood from the North Sea, they head to a potato farm at the foot of the Cheviot Hills that grows nine different varieties of potato.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree spills the beans on 10 things she loves about her husband, Ladd, and the food he loves most; Spatchcock barbecue chicken with apple brown sugar barbecue sauce; waffle fries with sour cream and onion dip; spoon cake with gooey chocolate frosting.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree cooks Tex-Mex marvels and fills the freezer with family favourites; she makes a big pot of green chili pork stew and a batch of Fiesta Mac and Cheese; her All-Purpose Tex-Mex Veggies go in soft tacos, veggie taco salad and quesadillas.

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a tasty tikka roast chicken, a vibrant carrot and grain salad, a super-speedy crab spaghetti, and Jamies chocolate orange shortbread.

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a super-fast, stress-free sausage carbonara with a peppery punch. Theres also a spicy one-pot Thai red chicken soup; a scrambled egg omelette; and steamed pudding with a speedy, sticky marmalade glaze.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: For lunch alfresco, Inas made roasted asparagus and Prosciutto with easy hollandaise. Then theres a date with Tyler Florence, whos sharing the secret to making a perfectly cooked Porterhouse steak.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: After making tomato crostini and whipped feta for lunch, Ina learns the secrets of Thomas Kellers buttermilk fried chicken. Next thyme roasted marcona almonds are the perfect snack for cocktail time.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers make signature savoury parcels, kouign amann and two types of showstopping doughnuts, which they hope will secure them a place in the quarter-finals.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the quarter-finals and the remaining five are tasked with baking enriched sweet fruit loaves, an Eastern European cross between a bread and a pastry, and 36 showstopping doughnuts.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James arrives at the palace of Versailles. As well as beautiful grounds, it boasts an incredible market where James shops for ingredients for his sole Veronique.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James arrives at the Bois de Boulogne. It is race day at the Auteuil Hippodrome and James cooks leeks vinaigrette with salmon, and lamb cutlets with ratatouille.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Chef Grudge Match
programme.desc: Host Laila Ali challenges chefs to settle their disputes in a single round, head-to-head culinary battle. The winners walk out with $10,000 cash, a prized knife from the losing chefs knife roll and bragging rights for life!

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Santa Fe, N.M., blue corn enchiladas with red chile sauce and chile en nogada, in Portland, Ore., won ton soup, in Albuquerque, N.M., beef cheek manicotti and squid ink black pasta with calamari.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Santa Fe, N.M., ma hi ma hi and Caribbean goat stew, in Albuquerque, N.M., turkey avocado with green apple chili chutney and a Duke City Reuben.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: A bakery in Albuquerque, N.M. crafts a unique take on the Thanksgiving bird, turkey wild rice soup and pumpkin pie in Santa Fe, N.M., smoked turkey and beans in Kansas City, Mo.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, Ore., a cafe cranking out comfort foods like the heartless artichoke sandwich and a curried lentil dish, in Albuquerque, N.M., soul food with a New Mexico twist, in Kansas City, Mo., a barbecue and blues joint.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey heads to Charleston for epic low country chow. He takes on the Belt Buster Challenge, in which he must consume a 1 kg burger, 1.4 kg of nachos, tater tots, and a 340 ml milkshake in 20 minutes.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb visits Sleepy Hollow, where he tries the massive Headless Horseman Burger and takes on the Dawg House Challenge, in which a roll of the dice chooses 12 insanely topped hot dogs that he must eat in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Mommas Day, Floral Flavors and Cake Clinics
programme.desc: Its a busy Mothers Day at the bakery when the team runs out of a key ingredient. Plus, the Valastro clan gathers to celebrate Momma.

programme.desc: Buddy creates an intricate cake for Sofias graduation, and a colourful cake for the Indian Holi festival brightens the bakery.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Eateries in Key West, FL, include a little shack serving up New England seafood, standout fish tacos, a tiny food cart serving Asian-Caribbean fusion dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Waipahu, lechon special and pork adobo fried rice, in Kailua, red curry fish and Mandarin duck confit, in Kaneohe, pan-fried ono and ahi cakes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Portland, Ore., a chef who butchers, cures, preserves and smokes everything in-house, in San Carlos, Calif., a neighborhood spot featuring burgers, cheese steaks and signature sandwiches.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Sweet and savoury, this is fruit all the way, and anything goes. Ina is pushing the fruit boundaries by shaking up the classics, and getting the scoop on fruit dessert.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inspired by a recent trip to Florence, Ina is re-creating a fabulous Florentine feast for friends back home in East Hampton. First, theres a mozzarella master class with a local cheese maker.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas got vegetable ideas for every day of the week, when youre stuck in a vegetable rut. There are fantastic main courses, starters, salads and sides.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Dinner on day one of Ina and Jeffreys California trip is chicken with wild mushrooms and green salad. The fun continues with an invitation to cook baked goat cheese at Sinskeys vineyard.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is all about what professional cooks make at home. Fantastic quick and easy recipes and top tips from the chefs and cooks in the know, for us to try at home.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Garten shares a collection of restaurant-inspired dishes to cook at home. Ina cooks with Grand Pellegrino for the famous Raos restaurant and finds out the best kitchens in America.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up some versatile meatball kebabs, delicious crispy pork noodles with sticky ginger and garlic, a magnificent carrot puff pastry and a humble crumble, all for under a quid per portion.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes sticky toffee pudding with his mum Sally. He also rustles up the perfect steak and onion sandwich, and cooks an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie takes on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat: chicken Kiev. He also cooks spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist, and creates a deluxe shepherds pie - made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder instead of mince.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernseys wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaires yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Its a trip back in time for Si as the Bikers cook dishes from his home county, Northumberland. This means a celebration of local seafood, a traditional lamb recipe and a new way of serving his favourite ice-cream.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Bikers cook dishes to feed body and soul, including some of their favourite recipes which are both healthy and delicious. Theres a wholesome warming soup, a chic healthy pudding and even a low fat version of burger and fries.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Bikers have a fresh take on pub grub with a new look for a traditional pudding, their take on the North-Easts mighty chicken Parmo and reveal the secrets behind their ultimate fishcake.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: The Bikers start their adventure in south Wales, hunting for unique ingredients to include on the menu of a family restaurant that has its eyes on winning a Michelin star.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adams culinary crusade takes him on a road trip along the beautiful Gulf Coast in search of delicious eats. Adam begins his journey at The Shed in Ocean Springs, Mississippi.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richmans tasty travels have brought him to the Florida Keys, an archipelago at the southernmost tip of the continental United States. The Florida Keys is the fishing capital of the world.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Adam tries Aberdeen Angus at one of the citys finest steak restaurants. Later, he checks out meat pies from an award-winning local butcher.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: In the Peak District, Adam learns more about the accidental invention of Bakewell pudding. Then, he visits a gastropub for his first taste of local grouse.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Fieris on a tasty tour of the Southeast, starting with an out-of-bounds brisket poboy in Port Allen, LA. Then, Guy finds scrumptious shrimp rolls in Gainesville, FL, and a Korean-Mexican mashup in Atlanta is rocking big-time bulgogi cheesesteaks.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Fieris diving into barbecue fusion and more! Texas meets Ethiopia at a Dallas-area joint with big-time brisket injera nachos. A Naples, FL, spot has a gluten-free twist on beignets, and an Atlanta chef is serving up craveable carnitas and barbacoa.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris digging cheffed-up eats of all kinds! A DDD alum is sourcing local Decatur, GA, ingredients for coffee-dusted venison, a joint in Texas is topping their bagels with basil schmears.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Britains Newsroom

programme.desc: Every weekday from midday, we bring you the news as it breaks. Wherever its happening, well have it covered - as well as giving our own view and how it matters to you with Tom and Emily.

programme.desc: Martin gives an up-to-the-minute take on the days events delivered in his own unique and lively style.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emilys David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britains food heroes and shes using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs need to impress the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate. They are joined by one half of the synthpop duo The Communards, Reverend Richard Coles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom `Westy Westerland is a former National Chef of Wales and head chef of the Brasserie at Lucknam Park in Wiltshire. Cindy Challoner is the former head chef at prestigious teaching restaurant `The Classroom in Cardiff. They face Andy Sheriden formerly of Sosban restaurant in Llanelli, returning to the competition for his second attempt at making it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding puree, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westys beef dish `The Cow Shed is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Wales must cook their four course menus again. They must take on board their veterans advice as they serve their tributes to Britains music success.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs begin by cooking their starters for a surprise veteran of the competition. Chris dish `Brixton Academy Through the Years is his refined take on the classic lamb, post gig junk food he ate after nights out at Brixton Academy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris is cooking his interpretation of the classic beef dish Tournedos Rossini using guinea fowl. His inspiration is Proms in the Park and the British artists who mix classical and modern music styles. Alexs dish commemorates his late brother and the song by Northern Ireland artist DonnaTaggart that he associates with the difficult time of his death.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Northern Ireland will recreate their four course menu for the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver. Theres also a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs battle to get their starter course to the Abbey Road banquet and dont disappoint with stunning dishes dedicated to everything from Tom Jones to Grime. The rivals are cooking to impress food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver, plus guest judge and British music legend Martin Kemp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs battle to get their starter course to the Abbey Road banquet and dont disappoint with stunning dishes dedicated to everything from Tom Jones to Grime. The rivals are cooking to impress food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver, plus guest judge and British music legend Martin Kemp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Some of the finest chefs in the UK compete to cook a fish course worthy of serving at a banquet celebrating the brilliance of British popular music, held at Abbey Road Studios. Along with the shows panel of expert judges, the rivals need to impress guest judge Kanya King.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: The Inca: Investigate the mighty Inca Empire and its accomplishments, including the construction of their awe-inspiring buildings in Cusco and Machu Picchu. (S2, ep 10)

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores British history through the stories of individual towns and examines Britain in wartime by looking at the coastal town of Dover.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Following the best marine salvage teams in the world as they race against the clock to recover ships wrecked by fierce storms that rage across the globe.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: A veteran comes to the rescue for a crew that has lost a valuable clam dredge in stormy waters. Unable to fish without it, their livelihoods are at stake.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeares first theatre in Londons Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeares rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britains most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the worlds first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the most exciting recent archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A team discovers clues to Scotlands first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure, and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Mysteries of the Worlds Oldest Temple
programme.desc: The ancient site of Gobekli Tepe is the oldest building complex ever discovered and continues to baffle archaeologists today. Now, experts investigate new theories behind its enigmatic construction as a possible commemoration to a cataclysmic event.

programme.title: Walking Through History

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Frost is under enormous pressure as he copes with the impending death of his wife. Meanwhile, the search for a missing child unearths evidence of a crime that took place thirty years earlier.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate unintentionally starts a chicken pox epidemic when she organises a mother and baby group, while Frank investigates a market traders van that has been hijacked and stolen on the moors, Lord Ashfordlys son is his main suspect. A local girl is pregnant and confides in Kate as to whom the father is.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass is spotted breaking into the Braithwaites barn, but Nick discovers that he has a legal order to clean the building. Elsewhere, an old sports car is buried under the hay bales and after much haggling Nick offers Greengrass a price they both agree on.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual Midsomer Regatta is brought to a standstill when a body is found in the river. It turns out to be that of a noted ladies man who had been previously seen arguing with friends. As Barnaby and Scott dig into the luxury lifestyles of the rowing set, they discover that money problems and sexual jealousy are rife.
