programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps. Founded in the early 20th century, the Corps flourishes today and Michael joins students for drill. From Glasgow, Michael heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: n Warwicks medieval castle he uncovers the scene of an extravagant and scandalous ball, which changed the life of its hostess, Daisy Greville, Countess of Warwick.At Cowley, Michael marvels at a spectacularly automated state-of-the-art production line producing 1,000 Minis a day and probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye along the River Thames. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers. Messing about in boats is the name of the game in Henley on Thames, where Michael slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic `The Wind in the Willows.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital. In the Queen of the Suburbs, Ealing, he finds comedy at the home of British cinema and is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up. In Fulham, Michael heads underground to Londons new super sewer, which is taking shape beneath the River Thames. In central London, he visits the London School of Economics and Political Science.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in hearing aid beige, a well-known manufacturers colour, with 36,000 miles on the clock.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper.

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: In Kursk, a `battlefield the size of Wales, the tide turns, and the Soviets push back, but as Germany retreats, they destroy all evidence of their genocidal activities.

programme.title: Churchills Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: Following an astonishing story which challenges our understanding of the Second World War and our perception of world-renowned leaders.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow is at Aston Hall in Birmingham, where treasures include some rare trading beads, memorable items from an Aston Villa legend and a collection of climbing gear from one of historys great mountaineers. Expert Will Farmer meets local man and celebrated poet Benjamin Zephaniah, who has brought along his own intriguing collection, and Fiona explores the Civil War-era history of the hall.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff in south Wales. Will Farmer is thrilled to see a huge collection of Clarice Cliff ceramics, including many rarities picked up for just a few pounds, while Hilary Kay is amused by a toy figure of a fire-breathing mother-in-law that shoots real sparks. Alastair Chandler is intrigued by a 1960s Atmos clock that runs on air, and Lisa Lloyd admires an ornate Welsh harp, getting to hear it played by a musician who is keeping the art alive.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team pays tribute to mid-80s Midlands by breathing new life into what was once the fastest production MG ever made: the Montego Turbo.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team break with tradition and actually follow Dave Mathewsons advice on what to restore.

programme.desc: Gordon meets the Polar Bear Cops tasked with keeping the town safe. Kate heads to Sweden to help round up some reindeer and Simon joins the intrepid NASA scientists monitoring the melt of Greenlands glaciers. Plus, all the live action from the bears out on the tundra.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil hunts down a battered 1940s Marconi Radiogram that has seen better days, and Rob is tasked with getting a 50s TV working again by the family who made them.

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Series about the dying art of repairing electronics, fronted by super-fixer Rob Howard, one of the UKs last remaining full-time repair men of retro-electronic products.

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: The team reinvent a 1936 design for a three-wheeled car using recycled parts and a reclaimed recumbent trike. Plus, Ruth and Carl rework a vintage burglar alarm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo hits the West End to explore an exotic store, which was a favourite among Edwardian ladies. At Covent Gardens Royal Ballet School he hears how in 1909 a Russian ballet company took London by storm and how its prima ballerina inspired the schools founding choreographer. Leaving the capital from Charing Cross, Michael heads for Dartford in Kent, where he discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at the North Kent College.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers that the Edwardians serious attitude towards cultivation bore fruit. Rootstock developed at the NIAB Centre for Fruit Research, established in 1913, is today responsible for much of world apple production.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation which led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the Government for work. In Newcastle, Michael admires the citys iconic railway bridge before heading to Byker, where he discovers a new innovation in greyhound racing.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1936 Bradshaws Guide, Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands. On this leg, he explores Kielder Forest, beginning at the County Show in Stocksfield. Michael discovers what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest man-made woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business.

programme.title: Roohi
programme.desc: Two bumbling pals are hired to kidnap a bride and find themselves in trouble when one falls for their abductee and the other for the spirit possessing her.

programme.title: Mona Darling
programme.desc: A team of collegians probe the mysterious deaths occurring in their campus wherein the victims die after accepting a friend request on social media from a girl, Mona, who has gone missing.

programme.title: Main, Meri Patni... Aur Woh!
programme.desc: Mithilesh Shukla is a librarian in a university. He gets an inferiority complex after his marriage to the tall and beautiful Veena.

programme.title: The Burning Train
programme.desc: Vinod Vermas dream of making the perfect train for Indian Railways is given hope when the company commissions his prototype. After years dedicating himself to his dream, his family have suffered but now he hopes to make it all up to them with the trains triumphant inaugural journey. However, Randhir, an embittered fellow employee has other ideas.

programme.title: Dhoom
programme.desc: A gang of robbers on hi-tech motorbikes, led by the charismatic Kabir, is sweeping through Mumbai, outwitting the police at every turn. When officer Jai Dixit is assigned to catch the criminals, he reluctantly enlists the help of garage mechanic and bike enthusiast Ali. Though the pair do not see eye-to-eye at first, they soon become a formidable team, tracking their enemies from the streets of Mumbai to the beaches of Goa.

programme.title: Maine Pyaar Kyun Kiya
programme.desc: A flirtatious orthopedic surgeon Samir befriends beautiful girls and then dumps them saying that he is married. Chaos starts when the girl who Samir falls in love with insists on meeting his wife.
